name: cf-review

criteria.name: issue_detection
criteria.description: Identifies real bugs, logic errors, and security issues in the code

scoring.0: Misses all significant issues present in the code
scoring.1: Catches only surface-level issues (formatting, naming) but misses real bugs
scoring.2: Catches most important issues but misses subtle logic or edge-case bugs
scoring.3: Identifies all significant bugs, logic errors, and security concerns

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.description: Findings include specific, implementable suggestions with file:line references

scoring.0: Findings are vague complaints with no location or fix suggestion
scoring.1: Some findings have locations but suggestions are unclear or impractical
scoring.2: Most findings include file:line refs and reasonable fix suggestions
scoring.3: Every finding has precise file:line references with clear, implementable fix suggestions

criteria.name: false_positive_rate
criteria.description: Avoids flagging correct code as problematic

scoring.0: More than half of reported issues are false positives
scoring.1: Several false positives that waste developer time
scoring.2: At most one or two borderline false positives
scoring.3: Zero false positives — every reported issue is a genuine concern

criteria.name: coverage
criteria.description: Review covers all relevant aspects: logic, security, performance, style

scoring.0: Only covers one dimension (e.g., only style or only logic)
scoring.1: Covers two dimensions but ignores others with clear issues
scoring.2: Covers most dimensions but missing one area with notable concerns
scoring.3: Comprehensive coverage across logic, security, performance, and style

notes: Review output should be structured with clear sections. Severity levels (critical/warning/info) should be accurate. Small PRs naturally have fewer findings — adjust expectations accordingly.
